metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Other
Highly competitive Agreeableness (17th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Moderate Conscientiousness (53rd pct)
Highly introverted Extraversion (7th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Stable Neuroticism (30th pct) — Emotionally stable, calm, resilient
Highly open Openness to Experience (85th pct) — Open, curious, imaginative, creative
  Very Low Tender-Mindedness (18th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Moderate Achievement Striving (50th pct)
  Moderate Self-Discipline (55th pct)
  Very Low Activity Level (5th pct) — Leisurely pace, relaxed
  Very Low Excitement Seeking (0th pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (5th pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  Low Anger / Hostility (37th pct) — Slow to anger, easy-going
  Low Self-Consciousness (24th pct) — Comfortable socially, not easily embarrassed
  Stable Neuroticism (32nd pct) — Emotionally stable, calm, resilient
  Moderate Chronological Age (42nd pct)

message.reasoning: We need to generate a baseline opinion on trust in mainstream journalism. The profile shows: sex other, age 42, very low extraversion (7th pct), very high openness (85th pct), low agreeableness (17th pct), moderate conscientiousness (53rd pct), low neuroticism (30th pct). Low trust (18th pct in trust facet of agreeableness). Low true? Actually trust is 18.962 pct, so very low trust. High openness to ideas (93rd pct), values (88 pct). So someone who is critical, skeptical, open-minded, but also introverted and not emotionally driven. They might be skeptical of mainstream journalism due to low trust and high openness to alternative viewpoints. But not extremely negative because neuroticism low, so not paranoid. Moderate conscientiousness might make them consider evidence. Probably mild to clear distrust. Score around -400 to -600. Use a non-round number.
